headline: Should FCC Approval for 248-Satellite Direct-to-Device Network Require Action From AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) Investors?
summary: AST SpaceMobile recently received Federal Communications Commission approval to modify its license and deploy up to 248 low Earth orbit satellites, enabling supplemental coverage from space directly to unmodified mobile devices across the U.S. using low-band 700 MHz and 800 MHz spectrum alongside partners Verizon, AT&T and FirstNet. This ruling not only validates AST SpaceMobile’s interference management and system design alongside terrestrial networks, it also strengthens its ability to...

headline: Bernie Sanders Says When Verizon CEO Predicts 20%-30% Unemployment Due To AI, People Should Pay Attention: 'We're Not Prepared'
summary: On Sunday, Bernie Sanders (I-Vt) raised concerns about artificial intelligence-driven job losses, pointing to a stark warning from Verizon's top executive. Sanders Amplifies Verizon CEO's AI Job Loss Warning Sanders highlighted comments from Dan Schulman, who in January warned that advances in AI and robotics could push unemployment levels to between 20% and 30% within the next two to five years. headline: A SpaceX Phone? Not if AT&T Gets Its Way.
summary: SpaceX won’t be an MVNO if  AT&T  has anything to do with it.  The acronym is short for Mobile Virtual Network Operator.  Think Mint Mobile or Cricket Wireless, companies that rent cell towers and wireless spectrum at wholesale prices.

content.title: A SpaceX Phone? Not if AT&T Gets Its Way.
content.summary: SpaceX won’t be an MVNO if  AT&T  has anything to do with it.  The acronym is short for Mobile Virtual Network Operator.  Think Mint Mobile or Cricket Wireless, companies that rent cell towers and wireless spectrum at wholesale prices.

headline: T-Mobile Stock Rises on Earnings Beat
summary: T-Mobile US  stock was up in late trading on Tuesday after the telecommunications company reported better-than-expected financial results.  “Q1 marked a strong start to the year as we continue to execute against our ambitious 2026 and 2027 targets, representing yet another proof point of our winning formula and unique differentiation,” CEO Srini Gopalan said in the earnings release.  T-Mobile  said it now expects 2026 net account additions to be between 950,000 and 1.05 million, compared with prior estimates of between 900,000 and 1 million.

content.title: T-Mobile Stock Rises on Earnings Beat
content.summary: T-Mobile US  stock was up in late trading on Tuesday after the telecommunications company reported better-than-expected financial results.  “Q1 marked a strong start to the year as we continue to execute against our ambitious 2026 and 2027 targets, representing yet another proof point of our winning formula and unique differentiation,” CEO Srini Gopalan said in the earnings release.  T-Mobile  said it now expects 2026 net account additions to be between 950,000 and 1.05 million, compared with prior estimates of between 900,000 and 1 million.

headline: As the Number of IoT Devices Starts to Surge, Verizon No Longer Relies on Traditional Subscriber Growth — VZ Stock Is Part of the AI Revolution’s Essential Nervous System
summary: While traditional logic suggests the U.S. wireless market is saturated, recent subscriber growth is decoupling from human population trends due to the explosive rise of IoT and autonomous machines. The "Big Three" are becoming the essential nervous system for the AI revolution, signaling a potential massive rerating of their historical valuation multiples.

content.title: As the Number of IoT Devices Starts to Surge, Verizon No Longer Relies on Traditional Subscriber Growth — VZ Stock Is Part of the AI Revolution’s Essential Nervous System
